mechanism: Visual stimulus (image, packaging design) is presented; observer estimates quantity from image anchor; actual consumption follows the anchored estimate without requiring complex inference. Heuristic judgment cascades to behavior via accessibility of anchor-consistent information.

Children receive multi-component literacy intervention (letter-sound, phonics, text reading, spelling) alongside standard instruction; outcomes measured via item-response theory; growth modeled over three measurement points; Tobit regression handles ceiling effects; effects compared across adaptive vs. fixed software conditions.

Harmonic tone (CS) is paired with spoken name (UCS) during acquisition; EEG records event-related potentials; P3 amplitude and time-frequency components (theta, alpha) extracted from fixed windows; conditioning indexed by CS+ vs. CS- differential responses; masking manipulation tests consciousness requirement.

Respondents report perceived fairness of government toward their ethnic group (ordinal survey response); response aggregated to country-year proportion; lagged proportion regressed on sustained growth indicator (binary: >=2% growth x 5 years); linear probability model controls for fractionalization, inequality, democracy, income.

Survey responses across 800 sites in 5 countries coded to 18 variables; factor analysis extracts simplicity, discipline, flexibility factors; regression models flexibility as function of simplicity and discipline; coefficients tested across countries; Variance Inflation Factors control for multicollinearity.

Participants interact with virtual stove or lights (repeated checking or repeated exposure condition); memory accuracy, confidence, vividness, detail measured post-stimulus; anxiety (SUDS, STAI-S) assessed at multiple time points; follow-up after delay; mixed ANCOVA with covariates (baseline anxiety, trait anxiety, checking symptoms).
